stance: Says Sparks has no money, blaming tax abatements given to companies on USA Parkway and an outdated property-tax system.
quote: the biggest issue I see is that Sparks has no money. I blame that on tax abatements being given out to companies that moved out to USA Parkway.

stance: Says cities cannot raise taxes, only fees, so revenue solutions must come through the state legislature.
quote: Because cities cannot raise taxes, they can only raise fees.

stance: Says Sparks is growing too quickly and, while it can't reverse, it should slow some growth.
quote: I think we're growing too quickly. We can't go back. But I think we can slow down some of the growth.

stance: Opposes the Red Hawk golf-course development and frames her campaign as people over the powerful, taking no developer or casino money.
quote: my campaign slogan is people over the powerful. And I have taken no developer money. I have taken no casino money and I'm not going to do it even when I get elected.

stance: Calls the property-tax system broken and antiquated, citing expenses rising far faster than revenue.
quote: there's no doubt it's our property tax system. It's broken, it's antiquated, it doesn't work.

stance: Notes police make up nearly half the city budget and that removing it would erase financial problems, contrasting with Clark County's voter-approved police tax.
quote: If we just take police out of our budget right now, that's $48 million out of a $120 million budget… we would have no financial problems.

stance: Says even unanimous local support for a police tax wouldn't matter because the state legislature controls whether the city can levy it.
quote: Even if we said today 100% of people in Sparks said, 'We want a police tax.' Guess what? Doesn't matter. State has to say we can do that.

stance: Urges residents to identify and contact their state legislators, who control city finances more than the mayor does.
quote: If you don't know who your state assembly person is or your state senator, find out, please. They control your life a whole lot more than the mayor of Sparks does.

stance: Promotes a 'lands bill' to grow Sparks eastward with mixed commercial and industrial development tied to the Tahoe-Reno tech jobs.
quote: The lands bill that I've been promoting for the last 12 years is grow east and mix more commercial and industrial out there, so that we're building the widgets that goes to the Tesla truck…

stance: Praises the city's HOPE team and CARES campus, arguing a regional HOPE team plus wraparound services is the path out of homelessness.
quote: The HOPE team is awesome. It's a model we're trying to get regionally… if there's a HOPE team from the city of Reno, Sparks, and Washoe County, we could be 24/7 and really making a difference.

stance: Warns that without a structural fix to Nevada's tax system, the city of Sparks risks insolvency.
quote: We're going to turn the keys over to the city of Sparks. We'll turn them over to the state because we're going to be insolvent.

stance: Agrees the number-one issue is the budget, with inflation driving up city costs while revenue lags.
quote: I would agree absolutely the number one issue we have had has been budget and like the mayor stated, those numbers of inflation has been our worst enemy in the last several years…

stance: Backs resetting property tax to market value at point of sale, framing it as a fair tax rather than a new tax.
quote: That's not a new tax, that's paying a fair tax of market value, whatever the value of the house is.

stance: Identifies as a fiscal conservative and says she declined a state-authorized pay raise that would have come from the city general fund.
quote: I'm a fiscal conservative. Every dollar I spend means a lot to me… I declined that raise which for me just on the city council was $40,000.

stance: Calls traffic from rapid growth the top issue, noting Sparks grew nearly 15% and infrastructure is strained.
quote: Traffic number one, absolutely number one is our traffic issue, which is growth… Sparks grew by a higher percentage than any of our neighbors in the area. Almost 15% growth out here in Sparks.

stance: Notes that pulling police from the budget would erase shortfalls but says cutting public safety isn't the answer; the city is short officers.
quote: they're short of police officers, they're short of fire people.

stance: Supports workforce housing for seniors, veterans and single parents, and partnerships with developers and the Reno Housing Authority.
quote: It's not low-income Section 8 housing. It's workforce housing that seniors that veterans that a single mom raising her kids can live in.

stance: Highlights the city's investment in the CARES campus and HOPE team community-policing model serving the homeless population.
quote: we invested CARES Act money to create that CARES campus to provide a facility for our homeless population, and we have been a huge supporter of that…

stance: Touts fighting a tax increase he says violated the Nevada Constitution, leading to a unanimous Supreme Court ruling and over $100M refunded.
quote: the courts refunded it after they determined at the United Nevada Supreme Court that the governor and the Democratic Party violated the Nevada Constitution, unanimously.

stance: Supports lands-bill development next to existing growth but warns high per-acre land prices undermine affordable, attainable housing.
quote: We need affordable attainable housing. And if you have to buy the land at that price, you're not going to have affordable attainable housing.

stance: Says meat prices are inflated by concentration in the packing industry, calling four firms controlling 85% a monopoly.
quote: you have four people that literally control 85% of the industry. That's not supposed to be allowed. That's called a monopoly.

stance: Argues Nevada is too reliant on California gas and should connect fuel pipelines, including a northern Nevada line to Utah, to lower costs.
quote: We are way too reliant on California gas… specifically here in northern Nevada pipeline to Utah makes some sense. A lot of sense, especially if you put it next to the existing natural gas line.

stance: Cites his natural-resources and water-law expertise as a distinguishing qualification for Congress.
quote: I am very well versed in the natural resources such as water law, which you know, nobody argues about water in state of Nevada at all…

stance: Frames the AG race around defending voting rights and reproductive health care against attacks.
quote: There are attacks on our right to vote, there are attacks on our reproductive health care and our freedoms and we really need somebody who understands those battles and is ready to take them on.

stance: Touts criminal and civil courtroom experience as the only candidate in the primary with legal trial experience.
quote: What I bring is that experience doing both civil and criminal litigation. I'm the candidate in the race in the primary… who has any sort of courtroom legal experience.

stance: Says building affordable housing requires fairness and a level playing field so all Nevadans have a fair shot.
quote: We've got to keep building affordable housing and a big piece of affordable housing is fairness, leveling the playing field and making sure that all Nevadans… have a fair shot of being successful.

stance: Says as treasurer he has fought federal overreach daily, arguing with the DOE and flagging unpaid federal bills.
quote: We have to fight back against this federal overreach and I've been doing that each and every day in the treasury. We're the ones arguing with DOE…

stance: Attacks his opponent for passing a bill he says lets NV Energy raise rates out of cycle, tying her to current demand charges.
quote: my opponent in this race actually passed a bill that allows for NV Energy to raise rates out of cycle. This whole demand charge thing that we're seeing right now, that came from her.
